name: Zambia

colors.symbolism: the country's natural resources, agriculture, and forests
the struggle for freedom and the blood shed for independence
the country's mineral wealth, especially copper

description: A green field with an orange eagle in flight in the upper fly, and three equal vertical stripes — red, black, orange — in the lower fly extending from the bottom edge to about two-thirds of the flag's height.
history: Adopted on 24 October 1964, the day Northern Rhodesia became the independent Republic of Zambia. The flag is unusual among national flags in placing its emblems off-centre at the fly. The eagle and the colours derive from the colonial-era coat of arms; the orange (originally a brighter copper-orange) was modified slightly in 1996.

features.symbolism: a green field charged with three vertical stripes — red, black, orange — in the lower fly, surmounted by an orange eagle in the upper fly; an unusual layout that places the heraldic devices off-centre
the people's ability to rise above the country's problems; taken from the national coat of arms

features.role: leftmost of three vertical stripes in the lower fly
features.symbolism: the struggle for freedom and the blood shed for independence

features.role: middle of three vertical stripes in the lower fly
rightmost of three vertical stripes in the lower fly
features.symbolism: the country's mineral wealth, especially copper
